title: Dashboard loading slowly — performance troubleshooting
content: A slow-loading dashboard is typically caused by querying too much historical data or having too many widgets on a single view. To improve performance: 1. Reduce the time window from 30 days to 7 days or 24 hours. 2. Use aggregated metrics (e.g., daily averages) instead of raw data points for long timeframes. 3. Break down large dashboards into smaller, focused ones (e.g., separate 'Database Metrics' from 'Frontend Metrics'). 4. Clear your browser cache or try incognito mode. If the issue persists across all users, check the CloudDash Status page for any ongoing platform incidents. This section provides additional context to ensure comprehensive documentation. The CloudDash platform is built with robustness and scalability in mind. When encountering issues, always verify your configuration settings and ensure that your network allows communication with our endpoints. If you continue to experience difficulties, please consult the troubleshooting steps outlined previously. Our support team is available to assist you with any advanced configurations or unexpected behavior. Remember to keep your agent versions up to date to benefit from the latest features and security patches. Furthermore, monitoring your usage limits can prevent unexpected service interruptions. For detailed API specifications, refer to our developer portal. We are committed to providing reliable service and continuous improvements to our platform. Should you have any feedback or feature requests, do not hesitate to reach out. Thank you for choosing CloudDash as your monitoring solution. We hope this guide has been helpful in resolving your query.
